subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Taylor / Maclaurin Series
stimulus:
A function f has derivatives of all orders for −1 < x < 1. The derivatives of f satisfy the conditions above.
The Maclaurin series for f converges to f

parts.prompt:
Show that the first four nonzero terms of the Maclaurin series for f are x − + − , and write the
2 3 4
general term of the Maclaurin series for f.

Determine whether the Maclaurin series described in part (a) converges absolutely, converges
conditionally, or diverges at x = 1. Explain your reasoning.
x
Write the first four nonzero terms and the general term of the Maclaurin series for g(x) = ∫ f(t ) dt.
